title: dodecatheon jeffreyi subsp. pygmaeum
intro: Dodecatheon jeffreyi subsp. pygmaeum, also known as the pygmy shooting star, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the gentian family (Gentianaceae) that is native to the Pacific coast of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dodecatheon jeffreyi subsp. pygmaeum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide antioxidant protection. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its flowers or leaves in hot water, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
